content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 17,
1992
Honorable Barbara
Lee
Member of the Assembly
p. o.
Box 20767
Oakland,
CA
94620
Re:
Confirmation of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A—92--130
Dear Assemblywoman Lee:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice I gave you
on February 21,
1992 concerning your
duties under the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”)
1
Specifically
you request confirmation of advice given on two
questions:
(1)
May
YOU expend campaign funds for the expenses incurred
in bringing children
from your district to the Capitol to attend a
legislative event recognizing the achievements of the Golden State
Warriors basketball team?
(2)
May you receive campaign contributions for the specific
purpose of paying the expenses described in paragraph
(1)
above?
This is to confirm that it is permissible under the Act for
you to expend and receive campaign funds to pay for the expenses
described in paragraph
(1)
above.
Section 89513(f) (1) prohibits
the use of campaign funds
“to make personal gifts unless the gift
is directly related to a political,
legislative or government
purpose.”
The event for which the children will be transported to
the Capitol is
a legislative event.
Also,
since the event
involves your constituents or the children of your constituents,
it is political
in nature.
Consequently,
even though the
expenditure of campaign funds to transport the children to the
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regu1atjon
428J Street, Suite 800
• P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916)322566O


Our File No.
A-92-130
Page
2
event would be in the nature of a prorata personal gift to each
child,
since the event is for a legislative and political purpose
the expenditure is permissible.
Furthermore,
given the political
nature of the expenditure, receipt of monies by your campaign for
the purpose of paying it is
a contribution which your campaign may
permissibly receive.
(See Section 82015.)
Should you have further questions please contact me at
(916)
322—5901.
Acting General Counsel
SH:aa

(2) May you receive campaign contributions for the specific purpose of paying the expenses described in paragraph (1) above? This is to confirm that it is permissible under the Act for you to expend and receive campaign funds to pay for the expenses described in paragraph (1) above. Section 89513(f) (1) prohibits the use of campaign funds “to make personal gifts unless the gift is directly related to a political, legislative or government purpose.” The event for which the children will be transported to the Capitol is a legislative event.
